factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in e-commerce and residential shipments.

evidence.text: Given these trends, overall volume grew moderately during the first quarter of 2016, with those products mostly aligned with the retail and e-commerce experiencing the most growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key segments, indicating strong growth.

factors.summary: Pricing power is holding, with base rate increases offset by lower fuel surcharge rates and customer mix changes.

evidence.text: Overall revenue per piece decreased 1.3% for the first quarter of 2016 compared with the same period of 2015, primarily due to lower fuel surcharge rates as well as changes in customer and product mix. These factors were partially offset by base rate increases.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is holding despite some offsetting factors.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards premium products and international segments, which is beneficial.

evidence.text: The shift in product mix towards premium express products offset declines in standard products. Additionally, the company experienced solid growth in its premium express services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards premium products and international segments is beneficial.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by volume increases and rate actions.

evidence.text: Total volume increased in the first quarter of 2016 compared with 2015, primarily due to continued growth in e-commerce and one additional operating day.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is accelerating due to volume and rate increases.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in e-commerce and residential shipments.

evidence.text: The increase in ground volume in the first quarter of 2016 was driven by both business-to-consumer and business-to-business shipping activity. The growth was fueled by e-commerce, which resulted in increased use of our returns and SurePost services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in e-commerce and residential.

factors.summary: E-commerce and residential shipments are growing fastest, while industrial manufacturing and professional services are slowing.

evidence.text: Business-to-consumer shipments, which represent approximately 45% of total U.S. Domestic Package volume, grew more than 6% for the first quarter of 2016 due to increases in both air and ground shipments. UPS SurePost had strong volume growth in the first quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed momentum with growth in some sectors and slowing in others.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, are declining, but the company is offsetting these through productivity gains.

evidence.text: Total domestic fuel surcharge revenue decreased by $105 million in the first quarter of 2016 as a result of lower fuel surcharge rates caused by declining jet and diesel fuel prices; however, the impact of lower fuel prices was partially mitigated by pricing changes to the fuel surcharge indices, as well as the overall increase in package volume for the quarter.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Declining fuel costs are offset by productivity gains.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher compensation expenses and health and welfare costs, but these are manageable.

evidence.text: These cost increases were largely due to the additional operating day and higher employee compensation expenses, which were impacted by (1) an increase in average daily union labor hours (1.8%) to support volume growth; (2) an increase in wage rates (1%); and (3) an increase in health and welfare costs (due to headcount and contractual contribution rate increases to multiemployer plans).

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, though manageable.

factors.summary: Operating margins are expanding due to productivity gains and lower fuel costs.

evidence.text: Operating margin increased 50 basis points to 12.1%. Productivity improvements supported by continued technology deployment, combined with lower fuel costs resulted in a 1.9% decrease in cost per piece and more than offset the decline in revenue per piece, which was mainly driven by lower fuel surcharge rates.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins are expanding due to productivity gains and lower fuel costs, indicating significant improvement.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, particularly in fuel and network optimization.

evidence.text: These cost increases were partially offset by a $21 million reduction in the cost of operating our domestic integrated air and ground transportation network, primarily due to lower fuel costs.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives have delivered measurable savings, particularly in fuel and network optimization.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through technology deployment and network optimization.

evidence.text: Productivity improvements have continued to be realized through adjusting our air and ground networks to better match volume levels and utilizing technology to increase package sorting and delivery efficiency. The continued deployment of ORION has contained the growth of average daily vehicle miles driven, while the increased redirect of SurePost volume to optimize delivery density on UPS vehicles has reduced the delivery costs for business-to-consumer shipments.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements through technology deployment and network optimization are evident, leading to efficiency gains.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is improving through network adjustments and technology improvements.

evidence.text: Our broad portfolio of product offerings and the flexibilities inherent in our transportation network have helped us adapt to these changing trends. The continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Supply chain health is improving with network adjustments and technology improvements, enhancing capacity and efficiency.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is improving as the company adjusts its network to match volume levels.

evidence.text: We continued to adjust our air capacity and cost structure in our transportation network to better match the prevailing volume levels.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Capacity utilization is improving as the company adjusts its network to match volume levels, indicating efficiency gains.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, particularly in technology and facility automation.

evidence.text: The continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are increasing in technology and facility automation, suggesting investment in future growth.

factors.summary: The economic environment is mixed, with modest U.S. growth offset by global weakness.

evidence.text: The U.S. economic environment has continued to be mixed as relatively stable consumer conditions are somewhat offset by continued declines in industrial production, soft business investment and higher inventory levels. While the worldwide economic environment has remained challenging in 2016, we have continued to undertake several initiatives in the U.S. and internationally to (1) improve the flexibility and capacity in our transportation network; (2) improve yield management; and (3) increase operational efficiency and contain costs across all segments.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The economic environment is mixed, with modest U.S. growth offset by global weakness, leading to a neutral assessment.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting international revenue and expenses.

evidence.text: Currency exchange rate movements had a significant impact on international revenue and expenses, with a net currency benefit of $3 million in the first quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements provided a net benefit, indicating a positive impact on financials.

factors.summary: Management is confident, with investments in technology and network optimization.

evidence.text: We believe that these adjusted measures provide meaningful information to assist investors and analysts in understanding our financial results and assessing our prospects for future performance.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management confidence is supported by investments in technology and network optimization, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include economic weakness in certain regions and fluctuating fuel prices.

evidence.text: Much of the weakness outside of the U.S. stems from difficulties in emerging markets, while developed nations experience solid growth. The uneven nature of economic growth worldwide has continued shifting of trade patterns and weakened demand in certain trade lanes.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include economic weakness in emerging markets and fluctuating fuel prices, posing challenges.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on technology, network optimization, and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Long-term strategy focuses on significant technology improvements and efficiency gains, indicating strong positive developments.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices and economic conditions.

evidence.text: The change in overall revenue was impacted by the following factors in 2016 compared with the corresponding period of 2015: Volume, Rates / Product Mix, Fuel Surcharge, Currency. Total Revenue Change Net Revenue Change Drivers: First quarter 2016 vs. 2015 4.5 % (0.2 )% (1.2 )% 3.1 %

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include fuel prices and economic conditions, which are risks but not clearly positive or negative.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new product launches and technology implementations.

evidence.text: Most notably, the continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Catalysts include technology implementations that improve efficiency, indicating positive developments.

factors.summary: Differentiation is achieved through technology, scale, and customer relationships.

evidence.text: Our broad portfolio of product offerings and the flexibilities inherent in our transportation network have helped us adapt to these changing trends.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Differentiation through technology, scale, and customer relationships highlights competitive strengths.

factors.summary: R&D investment is focused on technology improvements and automation.

evidence.text: The continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: R&D investment in technology and automation supports future efficiency and capacity improvements.

factors.summary: Digital automation is driving efficiency through initiatives like ORION and facility automation.

evidence.text: The continued deployment of technology improvements (including several facility automation projects and the accelerated deployment of our On Road Integrated Optimization and Navigation system - 'ORION') should continue to increase our network capacity and improve operational efficiency, flexibility and reliability.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Digital automation initiatives like ORION significantly enhance efficiency and capacity, indicating very positive developments.

factors.summary: New products like UPS SurePost are performing well.

evidence.text: UPS SurePost had strong volume growth in the first quarter of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows strong volume growth for UPS SurePost, indicating positive performance.
